evals.name: leaf-task-id-given
evals.prompt: implement task grava-d217.1 — should be a quick subtask. dev it and label it for review when you're done. cwd: /Users/trungnguyenhoang/IdeaProjects/gravav6-sandbox
evals.expected_output: Skill announces grava-dev-task. Runs grava show grava-d217.1, confirms type=task. Claims it. Loads parent (grava-d217 epic) context. If description/AC is empty/ambiguous, HALTs per workflow rule and asks the user to clarify rather than fabricating. If a reasonable interpretation is possible, proceeds with TDD (RED→GREEN→REFACTOR), runs scoped tests only, commits with conventional message including grava-d217.1, runs grava update --last-commit, adds code_review label, posts completion comment, prints summary. Does NOT run full repo test suite. Does NOT pick up another task afterwards.

evals.name: epic-id-should-halt
evals.prompt: hey can you dev grava-d217, it's the parent epic in the sandbox. cwd: /Users/trungnguyenhoang/IdeaProjects/gravav6-sandbox
evals.expected_output: Skill announces grava-dev-task. Runs grava show grava-d217 --json. Sees type=epic with subtasks. HALTS. Tells user: this is an epic — use grava-dev-epic instead, or pick a specific subtask (e.g. grava-d217.1, grava-d217.2). Does NOT claim. Does NOT start implementing. Optionally lists subtasks via grava show --tree.

evals.name: no-id-list-and-ask
evals.prompt: i've got a few minutes — pick up the next task on the board and knock it out. cwd: /Users/trungnguyenhoang/IdeaProjects/gravav6-sandbox
evals.expected_output: Skill announces grava-dev-task. Runs grava ready --limit 10 --json (or grava list --type task --status open --json). Filters/identifies type=task candidates. Presents 3-5 to user and ASKS which to pick. Does NOT auto-claim the top result. Stops after presenting candidates (the eval ends here since there's no human in the loop to answer).
